channel.title: Know Parenting
channel.description: Know Parenting is the gentle audio guide for tired, loving parents of children aged 0–8. Host Ruby An — alongside her husband Noah (a history lover) and child psychologist Dr. Psyche — turns child-development science into 3-minute calming episodes you can use tonight. From sleep and the growing brain to sensory storms and wired-differently kids, every episode pairs a true story from history with warm, judgment-free guidance. No guilt. No racing the milestone clock.
channel.itunes:author: Ruby An

item.title: Episode 001: The Brain That Grows While They Sleep
item.description: While your toddler sleeps, their brain is busy building itself. In this debut episode of Know Parenting, Ruby An, Noah, and child psychologist Dr. Psyche explore the 1953 Chicago discovery of REM sleep and what it means for your child's memory, mood, and development. A calming, science-backed wind-down for tired parents.

item.title: Episode 002: The Sensitive Window
item.description: Is your child 'missing the window' for reading or music? In Episode 002, we meet Emily and her block-stacking son Julian, then travel to 1907 Rome to witness Maria Montessori observe a child's 'sensitive period' for the first time. Learn why learning isn't a race — and how your child's mind has its own rhythm.

item.title: Episode 003: The Copycat Neuron
item.description: Why does your child imitate everything you do — the good and the difficult? In Episode 003, Ruby An and Dr. Psyche explore mirror neurons: the brain's built-in wiring to copy you. Understanding this connection lets you teach peace without saying a word. A calming, judgment-free guide.

item.title: Episode 004: The Sacred Space of Play
item.description: Do you feel guilty for 'just letting them play'? Episode 004 lifts that weight. Ruby An and Noah revisit a rainy-spring-afternoon truth: unstructured play is not lost time — it is your child's mind at work. Release the pressure to monetize every moment.

item.title: Episode 005: The Rhythm of Peace
item.description: Frantic mornings, bedtime battles, meltdowns when plans change. Episode 005 explores the quiet power of predictability — how a simple rhythm becomes a sanctuary for your child's growing mind, and for you. Structure as love, not control.

item.title: Episode 006: The Emotional Bridge
item.description: You love them completely, yet something feels disconnected. Episode 006 explores emotional attunement — learning your child's emotional language and building a bridge of safety and trust that lasts a lifetime. Connect before you correct.

item.title: Episode 007: The Screen as Bridge
item.description: Screens don't have to isolate. Episode 007 tells the story behind educational television and how a screen becomes a bridge to language and connection when children talk back, sing along, and share it with you. You are the anchor in their digital world.

item.title: Episode 008: The Sleep Factory
item.description: The nightly struggle for sleep — pleading for one more story, secret flashlight reading, exhausted mornings. Episode 008, 'The Sleep Factory,' helps you transform your evening routine into a sanctuary of rest and biological recovery. A gentle, sustainable wind-down.

item.title: Episode 009: The Language Bridge
item.description: Language isn't a race or a checklist — it's a bridge. Episode 009 travels to 1887 Tuscumbia, Alabama, to the story of a child finding words against all odds, and shows how one interactive moment at a time builds your child's growing mind. You are their first teacher.

item.title: Episode 010: The Temperament Compass
item.description: Your child isn't broken — they're wired a specific way. Episode 010 introduces the Temperament Compass: your child's behavioral style as a unique, hardwired map. Your role isn't to rebuild the compass, but to learn to navigate with it. No two compasses point the same direction.

item.title: Episode 011: The Wired Differently (Part 1)
item.description: Some children experience the world at full volume. Episode 011 begins our neurodivergence series: spotting the difference between a tantrum and a sensory meltdown.

item.title: Episode 012: The Sensory Storm: When the World Is Too Loud
item.description: Some children aren't throwing tantrums — their nervous system is drowning in sound, light, and touch. Episode 012 tells the true story of Eustacia Cutler and Temple Grandin, unpacks the science of the amygdala, and shares the Portable Sensory Toolkit. A calming guide for parents of neurodivergent kids.

item.title: Episode 013: The Language of the Body
item.description: Why fidgeting helps some minds focus. Episode 013 explores movement, stimming, and how the body regulates a busy brain.

item.title: Episode 014: The Predictable Day
item.description: Routine is a love language for neurodivergent kids. Episode 014 builds a gentle visual schedule that reduces anxiety before it starts.

item.title: Episode 015: The Deep Pressure Calm
item.description: Hugs that help, hugs that hurt. Episode 015 explores deep-pressure needs and safe ways to meet them.

item.title: Episode 016: The Social Map
item.description: Reading faces, taking turns, finding friends. Episode 016 offers a gentle path through social skill growth.

item.title: Episode 017: The Special Interest Spark
item.description: Trains, stars, dinosaurs — why intense interests are a strength, not a problem. Episode 017 celebrates the spark.

item.title: Episode 018: The School Transition
item.description: Starting school without losing the spark. Episode 018 prepares neurodivergent kids (and parents) for the big step.

item.title: Episode 019: The Advocacy Voice
item.description: You are your child's translator to the world. Episode 019 builds confident, kind advocacy for meetings and classrooms.

item.title: Episode 020: The Growing Up Wired
item.description: What wiring-differently looks like across the years. Episode 020 closes the series with hope, not fear.
